description: Constraint-based customer validation — Chapter 3. Persona, proximity, spending power, differentiation, and channel.

rules.name: No customer persona
rules.message: 👥 Describe your customer in at least one specific sentence — who are they, what is their situation, and what do they need from you?
rules.socratic_hint: The student hasn't described their customer clearly. Ask: who specifically would pay you? Name the type of person, their situation, and what they actually need.

rules.name: No proximity selected
rules.message: 📍 Select how close your customer needs to be — proximity defines your entire reach and go-to-market strategy.
rules.socratic_hint: The student hasn't defined the proximity of their customer. Ask: how far away can your customer be and still buy from you? This determines where you need to be found and how you'll reach them.

rules.name: No spending power defined
rules.message: 💵 Define what your customer can actually spend — this tells you whether your price point is realistic for this person.
rules.socratic_hint: The student hasn't defined what their customer can spend. Ask: does your target customer have disposable income for this? Would they comfortably spend $10, $50, or $200 on this type of product or service?

rules.name: Competing on price alone
rules.message: 💡 Being cheaper is the weakest reason to buy — a bigger business can always undercut your price. Pick a stronger edge: faster, more personal, more local, or better quality.
rules.socratic_hint: The student's only differentiator is being cheaper. Ask: what can you do that a large competitor cannot — faster turnaround, more personal service, more local knowledge, or something specifically tailored to this exact customer?

rules.name: No channel to reach customer
rules.message: 📣 Knowing who your customer is means nothing if you have no specific way to get in front of them — pick a concrete channel.
rules.socratic_hint: The student has no channel to reach their customer. Probe: where does your customer spend time physically or online? Is there a place — a local group, a platform, a street — where you can find them and make an offer?
